section.heading: Reports on reciprocity for security clearances inside of departments and agencies

subsection.heading: Reports to Security Executive Agent
subsection.chapeau: The head of each Federal department or agency shall submit an annual report to the Security Executive Agent that, with respect to the period covered by the report—

paragraph.content: identifies the number of individuals whose security clearances took more than 2 weeks for reciprocity recognition after such individuals move to another part of such department or agency; and
breaks out the information described in paragraph (1) by type of clearance and the reasons for any delays.

content.p: Not less frequently than once each year, the Security Executive Agent shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees and make available to industry partners a report that summarizes the information received pursuant to subsection (b) during the period covered by such report.

note.heading: Definitions
note.p: For definitions of “Security Executive Agent”, “reciprocity”, and “appropriate congressional committees”, referred to in text, see section 3352 of this title.
